It's a premium ramyun
There's spicy
And mild
Let's do spicy first
This has 4 packs each
With broth that was cooked for 20 hours,
And wind-dried noodles
The Mishik Master Ramyun
I'll bring some water
[Hot water]
[Electric water kettle]
The instructions...
500ml of water to boil
Put the flakes and noodles at the same time Cook for 4 mins and 30 sec
The noodles look shiny

It's dried with wind

Shiitake mushrooms,

Seasoning paste
[Like a true master chef]
[A shopping bag as a lid]

Looks tasty
I have some kimchi here too

Bon appetit
They say that dried noodle is a healthier choice
I'm curious
That's good!
I thought dried noodles would have a weak consistency, but it's very chewy
And it soaked up all the flavors
The soup is soaked in
Some soup
This doesn't taste like an instant-artificial food
It has some deep flavors
It's not too salty nor spicy
It has lots of different flavors to it
With some kimchi
Tzu fuel time
[Say "ah~"]
Ramyun is the truth

Let's wrap it with scallion kimchi
Lovely
[Be careful... You might eat the bowl too]
The noodles are not getting soggy either
It should be getting very bloated and soggy by now
It's not like that
[With some kimchi]
[And some scallion kimchi]
Jingu, are you not hungry?
As for the soup,
It really doesn't taste artificial
It's full of flavors
Very flavorful
[Are you going to eat or are you going to talk?]
Some say to dump the soup out because it's unhealthy
But these feel like it's okay to drink

Relieving
The flakes are huge too
The more noodles you cook, the less water you need
It will literally flood if you put 3000ml of water for 6 packs (500ml per noodle pack)
You're better off starting with less water and adding more as you cook
[6 packs of noodles gone just like that]
The noodles are supposed to be very soggy by now
But it's still fresh
[Let's get all the soup]
[Clean]
I have the rest in here
Now I want to try the mild flavor
Waiting on the water to boil
[Drinking ramyun soup like water...]
I've been told now to drink ramyun soup
But these feel like it's okay because it tastes healthy

Normally there's seasoning powder on the rim, but this isn't like that

Mild flavor

[Looking much better than before]
[I approve of the visuals]

This tastes very refreshing
This is just like Korea's classic mild ramyun noodles
The aftertaste is like black peppers and chili peppers
This is flavorful too
Looks good Cooked very well
Ta-da
I was not a fan of dried-noodles
But I'm loving the consistency of these noodles
This is not spicy at all, so it's perfect for those who can't handle spicy
Most mild ramyun noodles feel like the noodles are completely separated from the soup
But these noodles soaked up the flavors
These noodles were made with broth and not with plain water
I think that's why the noodles are so flavorful
This is going to be great with some kimchi
With some scallion kimchi
[She may or may not be teasing...]

It goes so well with scallion kimchi
My mom started to cook at home and she always thinks about 'wellbeing'
"Not too much salt, not too much MSG"
She's very careful about all of that
But I saw these in her cabinet
And she told me these are healthy ramyun
I think these are great for those who want some ramyun noodles, but do not want something too artificial
These aren't too heavy either
It feels like I didn't eat anything
I'm loving the black pepper and chili pepper aftertaste
This doesn't feel too heavy
This calls for some rice
These came with their own rice
These right here
3 different kinds
White,

Buckwheat,
Oat,
Let's eat the standard white rice first
This is made with just water and rice grain
It has no artificial additives So these are real rice!
Let's try it
I brought some side dishes too
Egg roll, spicy fishcake, spicy dried squid, stir-fried anchovies, and spicy squid
These are perfect for rice
White rice
The grains are big
It doesn't smell like an instant rice bowl
It's like something straight from a rice cooker
[TzuPD wanted to capture the scoop...]
[A for effort...]
That's cooked so well

The rice is not breaking up
Some spicy raw squid
These grains are so lively
Not like an instant rice
Not crushed up or broken
This is it
This is great I'm going to need to buy some
I don't really cook rice at home because I'm lazy
But these feel like it's from the cooker So I think they're great
And these were made safely [In a room as clean as NASA Class 100]
At a very clean and safe environment [In a room as clean as NASA Class 100]
With some stir-fried anchovies
[I know it's good, but slow down a little...]
[Eating the side dishes with rice]
Scrape up all the rice
It scrapes well too

But these aren't stuck on the bottom like that
[Nom nom]
All gone

The Mishik Oat Rice
It's filled with oats
Cooked well
[Spicy raw squid again]
[Again, she may or may not be teasing...]
This tastes very home-cooked
I love dry rice
This is just a perfect amount
[Gather all the soup in 1 bowl]
[Focus on the rice]
Eating like this at home is the best

The expiration date is long too
It's because no additives were added
I was worried that the expiration date would be too short
1 last bite
[Oat rice clear]
[Crushed Harim The Mishik!]
I don't usually drink all the soup in ramyun
But I cleared this one
I would recommend this brand if you're craving some ramyun and if you want to make a healthier choice
